rationale: Determinism check is mathematically infeasible for host_model_receive_packet's spec. The ensures uses receive_packet, which is disjunctive: (A) when pre.received_packet is None, record the packet via SingleDelivery::receive; (B) when pre.received_packet is Some OR okay_to_ignore_packets(), drop the packet (post == pre, out == empty). Because okay_to_ignore_packets() unconditionally returns true (its body is `true`), Case B is always available even when pre.received_packet is None. Therefore for the same pre-state, two distinct r1, r2 satisfying the ensures can exist: one taking Case A (post.received_packet = Some(pkt), non-empty sent_packets) and one taking Case B (post == pre, empty sent_packets). host_state_common_postconditions does not break the tie (valid for both cases). det_host_model_receive_packet_equal demands strict equality on (sent_packets vec, ack dst/src/msg, post.view()), which fails across the two disjuncts and across arbitrary ghost ack values in Case B. Verified empi
